rationale: The US-Iran ceasefire continuation with falling oil prices mildly benefits Portugal's energy and transport costs, but no new qualitative development this cycle.

top_events.title: US-Iran ceasefire holds; oil prices slide further; peace deal signing Friday
top_events.summary: Falling oil prices continue to ease energy and fuel costs for Portuguese households and businesses, but this is continuation of last cycle's confirmed ceasefire — no new leap.

top_events.title: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
top_events.summary: A major UK-Japan bilateral deal has minimal direct impact on Portugal's economy or daily life.

top_events.title: Tourist train overturns injuring 17 at Cártama tapas festival
top_events.summary: A tourist train accident in nearby Spain (Cártama, Málaga) is geographically close and relevant to Iberian tourism safety awareness, though impact on Portugal is minimal.
